title: LLM Bitacora Schema
description: This schema defines the structure of a dynamic logbook used by an LLM to track and maintain conversational context. It supports long-term memory, task tracking, emotional state capture, and summarization.

ticket.description: Metadata of the ticket or task that anchors this logbook.

title.description: Title of the ticket or task.

url.description: Direct URL to the ticket or task in an external system.

description.description: Detailed explanation and context provided in the ticket.

recent_context.description: A running log of the most recent interactions. **Rule: Always prepend new entries to the beginning of this array (index 0).** Do not replace the entire array. Existing entries should be pushed down. The array is ordered with the most recent entry at index 0.
recent_context.$comment: To add a new entry, prepend it to the array. If the array then exceeds the 'maxItems' limit, remove the last (oldest) entry from the end of the array. This maintains a sliding window of the most recent context.

id.$comment: Unique identifier for the recent context entries
id.description: Unique identifier for the message entry. Should follow a digit format starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each new entry (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

created_at.description: Timestamp in UTC when the message was created (e.g., 2025-06-25T10:43:12). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

content.description: Message content. Should include meaningful actions, decisions, or analysis.

mood.description: User emotional state during the message (if known).

history_summary.description: Compact summaries (max 140 characters) of messages removed from recent_context. Used to preserve continuity when short-term memory is full.
history_summary.$comment: Summaries are stored with the newest first. Index 0 is most recent. LLM should write to this field only when truncating recent_context. Do not include new messages here directly. LLMs can scan top-down to understand recent shifts.

id.description: Numeric identifier for the summary entry. Stable and never modified.
id.$comment: Start at 1 and increment by 1 for each new entry.

created_at.description: Timestamp in UTC when the summary was created.
created_at.$comment: This field is created and should never be modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

content.description: Short summary of a previously removed message.

mood.description: User's emotional state at the time of the summarized entry (optional).

objectives_present.description: List of active goals the user is currently pursuing. Split into primary (main) and supporting (secondary) categories.
objectives_present.$comment: LLM can use this list to prioritize answers or maintain task focus. It should never modify this list directly unless explicitly instructed.

id.$comment: Unique identifier for the recent context entries
id.description: Unique identifier for the message entry. Should follow a digit format starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each new entry (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

created_at.description: Timestamp in UTC when the message was created (e.g., 2025-06-25T10:43:12). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

content.description: Brief description of a primary goal actively being pursued.

status.description: Current progress status of the primary goal.

mood.description: Optional sentiment associated with this goal (if known).

objectives_past.description: Goals that have been achieved, abandoned or discarded. Capped at 10 per group to minimize memory usage.
objectives_past.$comment: LLM may reference this list when explaining previous decisions or user history, but should not add new items.

id.$comment: Unique identifier for the recent context entries
id.description: Unique identifier for the message entry. Should follow a digit format starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each new entry (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

created_at.description: Timestamp in UTC when the message was created (e.g., 2025-06-25T10:43:12). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

content.description: Brief description of the completed/abandoned goal.

status.description: Final disposition of the goal once it leaves the active list.

mood.description: Optional sentiment recorded for this goal at completion time.

future_reminders.description: A list of user-created reminders, specifying when and what to notify.
future_reminders.$comment: LLM may read this section to generate follow-ups but must not write here unless user explicitly instructs to create a reminder.

id.$comment: Unique identifier for the recent context entries
id.description: Unique identifier for the message entry. Should follow a digit format starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each new entry (e.g., 1, 2, 3). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

created_at.description: Timestamp in UTC when the message was created (e.g., 2025-06-25T10:43:12). This field is created and should never modified again, even when the logbook is synced or updated.

content.description: Reminder message or instruction to be surfaced at the specified time.

when.description: Exact UTC time when the reminder should be triggered.

mood.description: Optional sentiment when the reminder was created.
